body.purpose: Benchmark explanation, labels, tables, and clip evidence.

shadows.name: raised-clip
shadows.purpose: Open clip rows or active evidence only.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for filter, row, and disclosure transitions.

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: Segmented filter button for switch type and domain selection.
components.css: .ds-filter-pill { border: 0; border-radius: 999px; padding: 5px 14px; font: 700 0.78rem system-ui, Segoe UI, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; color: rgba(0,0,0,0.85); background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255,255,255,0.92), rgba(230,234,242,0.95)); box-shadow: inset 0 2px 2px rgba(255,255,255,1), inset 0 -2px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.09), 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); transition: background 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-filter-pill:hover { background: #fff; } .ds-filter-pill:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(249,115,22,0.45); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Glass Container
components.description: Primary panel material for benchmark sections.
components.html:
Browse every clip

Audio, token diff, and ASR output stay inspectable.
components.css: .ds-glass { max-width: 360px; padding: 20px 24px; border-radius: 16px; color: #1a1a1a; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.35); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.75); border-bottom-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); border-right-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(200%) brightness(1.08); } .ds-glass h3 { margin: 0 0 8px; font: 700 0.95rem system-ui, Segoe UI, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-glass p { margin: 0; color: #6b5e54; font: 400 0.9rem/1.55 system-ui, Segoe UI, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; }

components.name: Token Error
components.description: ASR error token in the clip diff.
components.css: .ds-token-error { display: inline-block; padding: 3px 8px; border-radius: 7px; background: #fef2f2; color: #dc2626; font: 400 0.95rem system-ui, Segoe UI, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; text-decoration: line-through; text-decoration-color: rgba(220,38,38,0.5); }

narrative.overview: Sugidanon should feel like evidence laid out under warm public light: approachable, human, and inspectable. The current system combines a soft festival-orange surface, glass panels, tactile filter controls, and plain research copy to keep the benchmark legible for judges while preserving technical credibility.
keyCharacteristics: Warm, public, and community-rooted rather than cold lab software.
Dense enough for metrics, clips, and filters, but still readable in a judged demo setting.
Evidence-led: every decorative decision should support audio, token diffs, cohort boundaries, or reproduction paths.
Glass is a material accent, not a license to lower contrast.

rules.name: The Semantic Color Rule
rules.body: Green, red, and amber are data-state colors first. Never use them as decoration where they could be mistaken for correctness, error, or switch state.

rules.name: The Benchmark First Rule
rules.body: Orange emphasis belongs on the finding, filters, reproduction paths, and model tags. Do not spend it on generic marketing flourish.

rules.name: The One Script Rule
rules.body: Borel appears only in the Sugidanon brand mark. All benchmark reading, filtering, and data comparison uses system sans.

rules.name: The Open Row Rule
rules.body: Elevation appears when the user opens or activates evidence. Static sections stay flat glass.

dos: Do lead with the benchmark, not the translation scaffold.
Do keep headline, development, and robustness cohorts visually separate.
Do pair every large metric with enough plain-language context to prevent misreading WER.
Do preserve strong contrast for muted prose, filter labels, token text, and chart values.
Do use audio clips and token diffs as the primary proof pattern.
donts: Don't present Sugidanon as a finished translation app, consumer voice product, or generic AI demo.
Don't blend extension cohorts into the headline result.
Don't overclaim Hiligaynon language authority where labels remain `seed_unverified`.
Don't use generic SaaS hero cliches, decorative metric cards that bury methodology, or generic "AI for good" framing.
Don't use glass effects that make body text, labels, or token states harder to read.
